items.text: crates/aion-nif exists as a workspace member; Cargo.toml depends on beamr and aion-core only, with no dependency on the aion engine crate.
src/lib.rs contains only module declarations and re-exports, no logic.
The crate sets unsafe_code = "deny" at the root and inherits the workspace clippy lints.
TermError is a typed error enum (thiserror) describing a term-conversion failure, including the failing argument index where applicable, not a bare badarg.
NifDeclError is a typed error enum (thiserror) describing declaration/registration-set failures, including duplicate (module, function, arity).
FromTerm decodes a Term (with ProcessContext for atom resolution) into a Rust type, returning TermError on type mismatch.
IntoTerm encodes a Rust value into a Term, allocating on the process heap through the ProcessContext, returning TermError on allocation failure.
FromTerm and IntoTerm are implemented for signed and unsigned integers, floats, and booleans.
FromTerm and IntoTerm are implemented for binaries (Vec<u8> and String) and for atoms via a dedicated atom-name newtype.
FromTerm and IntoTerm are implemented for Option<T> (atom nil for None, the value for Some) and for a Result-shaped {ok, T} / {error, E} tuple.
FromTerm and IntoTerm are implemented for homogeneous lists (Vec<T>) over proper cons lists, and for string-keyed maps (BTreeMap<String, T>).
A round-trip test (encode then decode) holds for every implemented type across representative values.
Payload (aion-core) converts to and from a Term via the JSON content-type (term_to_value to serde_json::Value to Payload and the reverse), round-tripping losslessly.
A blanket FromTerm/IntoTerm is provided for any T: Serialize + DeserializeOwned, routed through Payload, so a NIF can be written over ordinary Rust structs.
The payload module is the only place aion-nif references aion-core's Payload and ActivityError.
Determinism is an enum with exactly Pure and SideEffectful variants.
The Nif descriptor records module name, function name, arity, the NativeFn shim, a dirty-scheduler flag, and a Determinism tag.
The engine's registration path can read a Nif's Determinism tag to choose binding mode (inline for Pure, activity-body for SideEffectful).
deterministic_nif takes a Rust fn/closure with FromTerm arguments and an IntoTerm return and produces a Nif tagged Determinism::Pure.
The generated shim checks arity, decodes each argument by position (reporting the failing index on mismatch), calls the body, and encodes the return — author code never sees raw &[Term].
The generated return encoding is heap-safe (uses the ProcessContext allocation path) and does not Box::leak in any author-reachable path.
A panic raised inside the author body is caught and converted to a typed NIF error rather than unwinding across the FFI boundary.
activity_nif produces a Nif tagged Determinism::SideEffectful with the dirty-scheduler flag set, whose body returns Result<T, ActivityError>.
There is no untyped declaration path: deterministic_nif yields only Pure and activity_nif yields only SideEffectful, so a side-effectful NIF cannot be expressed as an inline deterministic helper.
An ActivityError returned by the body is encoded so its retryable/terminal classification survives the boundary back to the engine.
Where suspension is needed, the crate exposes beamr's existing request_suspend / wake_with_result mechanism rather than introducing a new async model.
NifSet collects declared Nifs under a module name and produces the value EngineBuilder::register_nifs consumes.
NifSet rejects a duplicate (module, function, arity) at build time with a typed NifDeclError and never silently overwrites.
aion-nif does not call beamr's BifRegistryImpl::register itself; the descriptor set is registered by the engine's runtime module.
Any unsafe is confined to the raw module behind safe wrappers, each unsafe block carrying a // SAFETY: comment; no unsafe appears outside raw.
A minimal illustrative NIF set (at least one Pure helper and one SideEffectful activity) exercises the full declaration-to-descriptor path and asserts the determinism tags and dispatch shapes.
